purpose: PreToolUse hook on Bash. Blocks `rm` commands targeting dashboard progress directories unless the dashboard data has been archived first. Extracts the dashboard ID from the rm command, reads the dashboard's initialization.json to determine if a swarm is/was active (task field non-null) and the task name. Then scans Archive/ for any directory whose name matches either the dashboard ID or the task name (case-insensitive, sanitized). If no matching archive exists, blocks with a copy-paste-ready cp command (`cp -r {tracker_root}/dashboards/{id}/* {tracker_root}/Archive/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)_task_name/`). Allow-list: ide dashboard (permanent, never archived), no Archive/ dir absent of swarm data, dashboards with task=null.
gotchas: Pattern matches both `*rm*dashboards/*/progress*` and `*rm*dashboards/*` — broader rm sweeps caught too
Archive matching is fuzzy (case-insensitive + space-to-underscore + grep-i) — rename collisions possible
Skips ide dashboard — it's reserved for the IDE workspace, never holds swarm runs
If task name has special chars (slashes, etc.), sanitization may not match the archive dir name
patterns: Fuzzy archive matching by either dashboard ID or sanitized task name
Block message includes a ready-to-run cp command with $(date +...) substitution
